api: Dolibarr REST API ({base_url}/api/index.php, Restler-based; class source: htdocs/{module}/class/api_*.class.php)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every REST method (@url-annotated PHP method) on Dolibarr's 5 already-migrated object classes (api_thirdparties.class.php, api_contacts.class.php, api_products.class.php, api_invoices.class.php, api_orders.class.php, all fetched directly from Dolibarr/dolibarr's develop branch on GitHub) is enumerated below. Every other Dolibarr module (users, projects, warehouses/stock, bank accounts, expense reports, proposals/quotes, shipments, and any object this bundle does not already read) remains out of scope. New in this pass: `{resource}/{id}` single-record GET streams for all 5 objects, create/update/delete writes for all 5 objects, and validate state-transition writes for invoices and orders.

excluded.reason: lookup-by-email variant of the already-covered GET /thirdparties/{id} detail read; no new data, just an alternate key
lookup-by-barcode variant of the already-covered GET /thirdparties/{id} detail read; no new data, just an alternate key
irreversibly merges two thirdparties (invoices/events/orders/proposals reassigned, source record destroyed); a data-integrity-risk admin operation, not an ordinary reverse-ETL write
customer price-level assignment; a narrow pricing-configuration mutation with no legacy precedent
assigns a sales representative (internal Dolibarr user) to a thirdparty; a relationship-management mutation orthogonal to this bundle's core CRUD scope
removes a sales representative assignment; same relationship-management scope as the POST variant above

attaches a category tag; tagging sub-resource, same scope as the GET above
detaches a category tag; tagging sub-resource, same scope

attaches a supplier category tag; same tagging sub-resource scope
detaches a supplier category tag; same tagging sub-resource scope
